title: Evangelion Pilot as a host of kinds
display_title: Evangelion Pilot as a host of kinds
wikitext: []: Evangelion Pilot as a "host" of kinds?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Nov 19, 2013 7:36 am When I was looking at the newly added reference to Ultraman in the Wiki, I did take a look at some parts of the Ultraman series background - all these Japanese "special effect" movies are something totally beyond my taste. However, I did notice something interesting - that the Ultras are referred to as "beings of light", albeit with internal organs like human beings (some parts like that timer light being non-human) and capable of "bleeding" light. All of this, of course, ties in nicely to what we see in Eva 2.0 with Eva-01 pseudo-evolving into a giant of light. Come 3.0, we discover that Evangelion pilots aren't exactly "human" and don't age at all. This makes me wonder whether Eva-01 in Rebuild operates like this - 1. The Eva has a physical body that Nerv has heavily modified, however it also has a "giant of light" body that does not normally show. 2. An Eva is incapable of operation unless it has a human pilot as a 'host' for the "giant of light" being, and it is through the pilot that the Eva is controlled. It could also explain how Shinji, ostensibly a human, could have his eyes glow red in an out-of-battery Eva. He has become an unwitting 'host' or 'medium' for the Eva's "giant of light" body, with his mother acting as the physical body's control system. Shinji is still more or less a normal human being outside the Eva and without being synched to his mom inside - but maybe like Asuka or Mari he doesn't age any longer post-3.0. 3. Shinji has lost his ability to synch with Eva-01 in 3.0 because he no longer 'hosts' the "giant of light" body. Mind you, this still doesn't give a complete explanation as to how Mari operates multiple Evas, or what is going on with Rei Q and Mark. 09, but it's a start.

Article: Evangelion Pilot as a host of kinds
[]: Evangelion Pilot as a "host" of kinds?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Nov 19, 2013 7:36 am When I was looking at the newly added reference to Ultraman in the Wiki, I did take a look at some parts of the Ultraman series background - all these Japanese "special effect" movies are something totally beyond my taste. However, I did notice something interesting - that the Ultras are referred to as "beings of light", albeit with internal organs like human beings (some parts like that timer light being non-human) and capable of "bleeding" light. All of this, of course, ties in nicely to what we see in Eva 2.0 with Eva-01 pseudo-evolving into a giant of light. Come 3.0, we discover that Evangelion pilots aren't exactly "human" and don't age at all. This makes me wonder whether Eva-01 in Rebuild operates like this - 1. The Eva has a physical body that Nerv has heavily modified, however it also has a "giant of light" body that does not normally show. 2. An Eva is incapable of operation unless it has a human pilot as a 'host' for the "giant of light" being, and it is through the pilot that the Eva is controlled. It could also explain how Shinji, ostensibly a human, could have his eyes glow red in an out-of-battery Eva. He has become an unwitting 'host' or 'medium' for the Eva's "giant of light" body, with his mother acting as the physical body's control system. Shinji is still more or less a normal human being outside the Eva and without being synched to his mom inside - but maybe like Asuka or Mari he doesn't age any longer post-3.0. 3. Shinji has lost his ability to synch with Eva-01 in 3.0 because he no longer 'hosts' the "giant of light" body. Mind you, this still doesn't give a complete explanation as to how Mari operates multiple Evas, or what is going on with Rei Q and Mark. 09, but it's a start.
